name: Crayfish Etouffee
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy pot, melt butter and sauté onions, celery, green pepper, and garlic until soft.
2. Stir in salt, black pepper, cayenne pepper, sugar, and tomato paste. Simmer, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes.
3. Dissolve cornstarch in water; add white wine to the mixture. Cook for 20 minutes or until the sauce thickens.
4. Add crayfish tail meat, chopped scallion, and Kitchen bouquet. Stir to combine.
5. Refrigerate overnight. Reheat before serving over rice. Do not overcook.
6. To prepare crayfish: Discard any dead crayfish in the sack. Wash live crayfish in clean, cool water just before cooking. They do not need to be purged with salt during washing.
7. Place the live crayfish in boiling water and boil for 10 minutes (or until water returns to a boil after adding crayfish). Season the water with one pound of salt per five gallons of water, and add other desired seasonings such as cayenne pepper, garlic, onions, lemons, or lemon juice.
8. It is usual to add potatoes and corn on the cob to the boiling water. Crayfish are easiest to peel when still warm.
